introSection:
Goliath is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA. It opened on February 11, 2000, and was the fastest complete-circuit coaster in the world. That record, however, was quickly eclipsed by Millennium Force at Cedar Point three months later.

The ride is one of three coasters designed and manufactured by Giovanola. The other two are Titan at Six Flags Over Texas and Anaconda at Gold Reef City. It is almost identical to Titan, which opened a year after Goliath. However, Titan has an extra helix prior to the mid-course brake run and thus has a longer track length. Goliath is one of two hyper roller coasters in California, with the other one being Xcelerator at Knott's Berry Farm.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On January 29, 1999, track pieces arrived at Six Flags Magic Mountain. It was then confirmed that the new attraction would be a hyper roller coaster. Vertical construction officially began in April 1999.

On November 11, 1999, Six Flags Magic Mountain announced that the new coaster would be named Goliath.

The ride opened on February 11, 2000.

In November 2013, seatbelts were added to Goliath, along with Full Throttle.

The ride was repainted in 2025.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On June 2, 2001, a 28-year-old woman died after suffering brain damage while riding Goliath. While the ride was initially closed, it reopened later that day after an inspection. The following day, an autopsy failed to find a pre-existing condition and Goliath was once again closed for investigation. The ride reopened on June 14 after OSHA investigators deemed the ride safe to operate.

fullText:
Goliath is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA. It opened on February 11, 2000, and was the fastest complete-circuit coaster in the world. That record, however, was quickly eclipsed by Millennium Force at Cedar Point three months later.

The ride is one of three coasters designed and manufactured by Giovanola. The other two are Titan at Six Flags Over Texas and Anaconda at Gold Reef City. It is almost identical to Titan, which opened a year after Goliath. However, Titan has an extra helix prior to the mid-course brake run and thus has a longer track length. Goliath is one of two hyper roller coasters in California, with the other one being Xcelerator at Knott's Berry Farm.

On January 29, 1999, track pieces arrived at Six Flags Magic Mountain. It was then confirmed that the new attraction would be a hyper roller coaster. Vertical construction officially began in April 1999.

On November 11, 1999, Six Flags Magic Mountain announced that the new coaster would be named Goliath.

The ride opened on February 11, 2000.

In November 2013, seatbelts were added to Goliath, along with Full Throttle.

The ride was repainted in 2025.



Orange track and blue supports.



On June 2, 2001, a 28-year-old woman died after suffering brain damage while riding Goliath. While the ride was initially closed, it reopened later that day after an inspection. The following day, an autopsy failed to find a pre-existing condition and Goliath was once again closed for investigation. The ride reopened on June 14 after OSHA investigators deemed the ride safe to operate.
